response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced generalist with genuine developer-tooling interest — including direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test frameworks and language libraries. However, the role's core requirements are not met: there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, and Java appears only peripherally (robotics work in 2016-2018, a skills-list mention). His depth lies in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms rather than the enterprise Java ecosystem the position centers on.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling track record: test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators
Proven ability to master diverse stacks and complex systems, with craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework background
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms, far from JVM tooling
